title: PALM: Progress-Aware Policy Learning via Affordance Reasoning for Long-Horizon Robotic Manipulation
summary: Recent advancements in vision-language-action (VLA) models have shown promise in robotic manipulation, yet they continue to struggle with long-horizon, multi-step tasks. Existing methods lack internal reasoning mechanisms that can identify task-relevant interaction cues or track progress within a subtask, leading to critical execution errors such as repeated actions, missed steps, and premature termination. To address these challenges, we introduce PALM, a VLA framework that structures policy learning around interaction-centric affordance reasoning and subtask progress cues. PALM distills complementary affordance representations that capture object relevance, contact geometry, spatial placements, and motion dynamics, and serve as task-relevant anchors for visuomotor control. To further stabilize long-horizon execution, PALM predicts continuous within-subtask progress, enabling seamless subtask transitions. Across extensive simulation and real-world experiments, PALM consistently outperforms baselines, achieving a 91.8% success rate on LIBERO-LONG, a 12.5% improvement in average length on CALVIN ABC->D, and a 2x improvement over real-world baselines across three long-horizon generalization settings.
tldr: The paper introduces PALM, a Vision-Language-Action framework for robotic manipulation that uses affordance reasoning and subtask progress cues to improve long-horizon task performance, demonstrating significant improvements in both simulation and real-world experiments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了PALM，一个用于机器人操作的视觉-语言-动作框架，它使用可供性推理和子任务进度提示来提高长程任务的性能，并在模拟和真实世界的实验中展示了显著的改进。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作(VLA)模型近期的进展在机器人操作领域展现出潜力，但它们仍然难以处理长时程、多步骤的任务。现有方法缺乏内部推理机制，无法识别任务相关的交互线索或追踪子任务内的进度，导致关键的执行错误，例如重复动作、遗漏步骤和过早终止。为了应对这些挑战，我们引入了PALM，一个VLA框架，它围绕以交互为中心的示能推理和子任务进度线索来构建策略学习。PALM提取互补的示能表示，捕获对象相关性、接触几何、空间放置和运动动力学，并作为视觉运动控制的任务相关锚点。为了进一步稳定长时程执行，PALM预测连续的子任务内进度，从而实现无缝的子任务转换。在广泛的模拟和真实世界实验中，PALM始终优于基线方法，在LIBERO-LONG上实现了91.8%的成功率，在CALVIN ABC->D上平均长度提高了12.5%，并且在三个长时程泛化设置中，真实世界的性能优于基线方法2倍。

title: Evaluating the encoding competence of visual language models using uncommon actions
summary: We propose UAIT (Uncommon-sense Action Image-Text) dataset, a new evaluation benchmark designed to test the semantic understanding ability of visual language models (VLMs) in uncommon-sense action scenes. Unlike previous datasets that focus on common visual scenes with statistical frequency advantages, UAIT challenges models with grammatically reasonable but semantically counter-common sense image-text pairs. Such tasks require models to go beyond superficial pattern recognition and demonstrate a deep understanding of agent-patient relationships and physical feasibility. To build UAIT, we designed a semi-automated process to synthesize high-quality uncommon-sense image-text samples using large language models, few-shot prompt engineering, and text-to-image generation. Each sample is accompanied by a carefully designed multiple-choice question to test the model's competence in fine-grained reasoning. We evaluate multiple state-of-the-art visual language models and compare them with models based on contrastive learning. Experiments show that all models perform significantly worse than humans in semantic judgment, especially in distinguishing grammatical correctness from semantic rationality. Further experiments show that even the lightweight model can improve its accuracy after fine-tuning, demonstrating the great potential of directional adaptation. This study not only reveals the key weaknesses of VLMs, but also provides diagnostic tools and research directions for the development of robust models with real visual semantic reasoning capabilities.
tldr: The paper introduces UAIT, a new benchmark to evaluate VLMs' semantic understanding of uncommon-sense actions using synthesized image-text pairs, revealing weaknesses in semantic judgment and potential for fine-tuning improvements.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍UAIT，一个新的基准数据集，用于评估视觉语言模型对非常见动作场景的语义理解能力，通过合成的图文对揭示了模型在语义判断方面的不足以及微调改进的潜力。
summary_zh: 我们提出了 UAIT (Uncommon-sense Action Image-Text) 数据集，这是一个新的评估基准，旨在检验视觉语言模型 (VLMs) 在违反常识的动作场景中的语义理解能力。与以往侧重于具有统计频率优势的常见视觉场景的数据集不同，UAIT 使用语法合理但语义上违反常识的图像-文本对来挑战模型。此类任务要求模型超越表面模式识别，并展示对施事者-受事者关系和物理可行性的深刻理解。为了构建 UAIT，我们设计了一个半自动化流程，使用大型语言模型、少样本提示工程和文本到图像生成来合成高质量的违反常识的图像-文本样本。每个样本都附带一个精心设计的多项选择题，以测试模型在细粒度推理方面的能力。我们评估了多个最先进的视觉语言模型，并将其与基于对比学习的模型进行了比较。实验表明，所有模型的语义判断能力都远低于人类，尤其是在区分语法正确性和语义合理性方面。进一步的实验表明，即使是轻量级模型在微调后也能提高其准确性，这表明定向适应具有巨大的潜力。这项研究不仅揭示了 VLMs 的关键弱点，还为开发具有真正视觉语义推理能力的鲁棒模型提供了诊断工具和研究方向。

title: Hiking in the Wild: A Scalable Perceptive Parkour Framework for Humanoids
summary: Achieving robust humanoid hiking in complex, unstructured environments requires transitioning from reactive proprioception to proactive perception. However, integrating exteroception remains a significant challenge: mapping-based methods suffer from state estimation drift; for instance, LiDAR-based methods do not handle torso jitter well. Existing end-to-end approaches often struggle with scalability and training complexity; specifically, some previous works using virtual obstacles are implemented case-by-case. In this work, we present \textit{Hiking in the Wild}, a scalable, end-to-end parkour perceptive framework designed for robust humanoid hiking. To ensure safety and training stability, we introduce two key mechanisms: a foothold safety mechanism combining scalable \textit{Terrain Edge Detection} with \textit{Foot Volume Points} to prevent catastrophic slippage on edges, and a \textit{Flat Patch Sampling} strategy that mitigates reward hacking by generating feasible navigation targets. Our approach utilizes a single-stage reinforcement learning scheme, mapping raw depth inputs and proprioception directly to joint actions, without relying on external state estimation. Extensive field experiments on a full-size humanoid demonstrate that our policy enables robust traversal of complex terrains at speeds up to 2.5 m/s. The training and deployment code is open-sourced to facilitate reproducible research and deployment on real robots with minimal hardware modifications.
tldr: This paper introduces a scalable, end-to-end reinforcement learning framework called "Hiking in the Wild" for robust humanoid hiking in complex terrains, using depth inputs and proprioception, alongside safety mechanisms to prevent slippage and reward hacking. It demonstrates successful field experiments on a full-size humanoid at 2.5 m/s and provides open-sourced code.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个名为“野外远足（Hiking in the Wild）”的可扩展端到端强化学习框架，该框架用于在复杂地形中进行稳健的人形机器人远足，使用深度输入和本体感觉，并结合安全机制来防止滑移和奖励作弊。论文并通过在全尺寸人形机器人上的试验(速度高达2.5米/秒)验证了该方法，并提供了开源代码。
summary_zh: 在复杂、非结构化环境中实现稳健的类人步行需要从被动的本体感受过渡到主动的外感受。然而，整合外感受仍然是一个重大挑战：基于地图的方法受状态估计漂移的困扰；例如，基于激光雷达的方法无法很好地处理躯干抖动。现有的端到端方法通常难以扩展且训练复杂；具体而言，一些先前使用虚拟障碍物的工作是针对特定情况实现的。在这项工作中，我们提出了“荒野徒步”，这是一个可扩展的、端到端的跑酷感知框架，专为稳健的类人步行而设计。为了确保安全和训练稳定性，我们引入了两个关键机制：一个结合了可扩展的“地形边缘检测”和“足部体积点”的落脚点安全机制，以防止在边缘发生灾难性滑动；以及一个通过生成可行的导航目标来缓解奖励黑客行为的“平坦区域采样”策略。我们的方法采用单阶段强化学习方案，将原始深度输入和本体感受直接映射到关节动作，而无需依赖外部状态估计。在全尺寸类人机器人上的大量现场实验表明，我们的策略能够以高达 2.5 米/秒的速度稳健地穿越复杂地形。训练和部署代码已开源，以方便在硬件修改最小的情况下，在真实机器人上进行可重复的研究和部署。

title: Deep Whole-body Parkour
summary: Current approaches to humanoid control generally fall into two paradigms: perceptive locomotion, which handles terrain well but is limited to pedal gaits, and general motion tracking, which reproduces complex skills but ignores environmental capabilities. This work unites these paradigms to achieve perceptive general motion control. We present a framework where exteroceptive sensing is integrated into whole-body motion tracking, permitting a humanoid to perform highly dynamic, non-locomotion tasks on uneven terrain. By training a single policy to perform multiple distinct motions across varied terrestrial features, we demonstrate the non-trivial benefit of integrating perception into the control loop. Our results show that this framework enables robust, highly dynamic multi-contact motions, such as vaulting and dive-rolling, on unstructured terrain, significantly expanding the robot's traversability beyond simple walking or running. https://project-instinct.github.io/deep-whole-body-parkour
tldr: This paper presents a framework integrating exteroceptive sensing into whole-body motion tracking, enabling a humanoid robot to perform complex parkour maneuvers on uneven terrain, demonstrating robust multi-contact motions beyond simple locomotion.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一个框架，将外部感知融入全身运动追踪，使人形机器人能够在不平坦的地形上执行复杂的跑酷动作，展示了超越简单运动的鲁棒的多接触运动。
summary_zh: 目前人形机器人控制方法通常分为两种范式：感知运动，其擅长处理地形，但仅限于足式步态；以及通用运动跟踪，其能复现复杂的技能，但忽略了环境适应能力。本研究将这两种范式结合起来，以实现感知通用运动控制。我们提出了一种框架，将外部感知融入全身运动跟踪中，使人形机器人能够在崎岖地形上执行高度动态的、非行走的任务。通过训练一个策略来执行跨越不同地形特征的多个不同动作，我们展示了将感知融入控制回路的显著优势。我们的结果表明，该框架能够实现鲁棒、高度动态的多接触运动，如跨越和翻滚，在非结构化地形上，显著扩展了机器人的可通行性，超越了简单的行走或奔跑。

title: FlyCo: Foundation Model-Empowered Drones for Autonomous 3D Structure Scanning in Open-World Environments
summary: Autonomous 3D scanning of open-world target structures via drones remains challenging despite broad applications. Existing paradigms rely on restrictive assumptions or effortful human priors, limiting practicality, efficiency, and adaptability. Recent foundation models (FMs) offer great potential to bridge this gap. This paper investigates a critical research problem: What system architecture can effectively integrate FM knowledge for this task? We answer it with FlyCo, a principled FM-empowered perception-prediction-planning loop enabling fully autonomous, prompt-driven 3D target scanning in diverse unknown open-world environments. FlyCo directly translates low-effort human prompts (text, visual annotations) into precise adaptive scanning flights via three coordinated stages: (1) perception fuses streaming sensor data with vision-language FMs for robust target grounding and tracking; (2) prediction distills FM knowledge and combines multi-modal cues to infer the partially observed target's complete geometry; (3) planning leverages predictive foresight to generate efficient and safe paths with comprehensive target coverage. Building on this, we further design key components to boost open-world target grounding efficiency and robustness, enhance prediction quality in terms of shape accuracy, zero-shot generalization, and temporal stability, and balance long-horizon flight efficiency with real-time computability and online collision avoidance. Extensive challenging real-world and simulation experiments show FlyCo delivers precise scene understanding, high efficiency, and real-time safety, outperforming existing paradigms with lower human effort and verifying the proposed architecture's practicality. Comprehensive ablations validate each component's contribution. FlyCo also serves as a flexible, extensible blueprint, readily leveraging future FM and robotics advances. Code will be released.
tldr: FlyCo is a foundation model-empowered system architecture for autonomous 3D scanning of open-world environments using drones, demonstrating improved efficiency and safety compared to existing methods while requiring less human effort.
tldr_zh: FlyCo是一个基于基础模型的系统结构，用于无人机自主三维扫描开放世界环境，与现有方法相比，展示了更高的效率和安全性，同时减少了人为干预。
summary_zh: 尽管应用广泛，但通过无人机对开放世界目标结构进行自主3D扫描仍然充满挑战。现有的范式依赖于严格的假设或费力的人工先验知识，限制了实用性、效率和适应性。最近的基础模型（FMs）为弥合这一差距提供了巨大的潜力。本文研究了一个关键的研究问题：什么样的系统架构可以有效地整合FM知识来完成这项任务？我们通过FlyCo来回答这个问题，这是一个基于FM赋能的感知-预测-规划循环，能够在各种未知的开放世界环境中实现完全自主、提示驱动的3D目标扫描。FlyCo通过三个协调阶段，将低成本的人工提示（文本、视觉标注）直接转化为精确的自适应扫描飞行：（1）感知融合流式传感器数据和视觉-语言FM，以实现稳健的目标定位和跟踪；（2）预测提炼FM知识并结合多模态线索，以推断部分观测到的目标的完整几何形状；（3）规划利用预测性洞察力来生成高效且安全的路径，实现全面的目标覆盖。在此基础上，我们进一步设计关键组件，以提高开放世界目标定位的效率和鲁棒性，增强形状精度、零样本泛化和时间稳定性方面的预测质量，并平衡长时程飞行效率与实时可计算性和在线避障。大量具有挑战性的真实世界和模拟实验表明，FlyCo具有精确的场景理解能力、高效率和实时安全性，优于现有的范式，且降低了人工成本，验证了所提出的架构的实用性。全面的消融实验验证了每个组件的贡献。FlyCo还可以作为一个灵活、可扩展的蓝图，随时可以利用未来的FM和机器人技术的进步。代码将会发布。

title: OSCAR: Open-Set CAD Retrieval from a Language Prompt and a Single Image
summary: 6D object pose estimation plays a crucial role in scene understanding for applications such as robotics and augmented reality. To support the needs of ever-changing object sets in such context, modern zero-shot object pose estimators were developed to not require object-specific training but only rely on CAD models. Such models are hard to obtain once deployed, and a continuously changing and growing set of objects makes it harder to reliably identify the instance model of interest. To address this challenge, we introduce an Open-Set CAD Retrieval from a Language Prompt and a Single Image (OSCAR), a novel training-free method that retrieves a matching object model from an unlabeled 3D object database. During onboarding, OSCAR generates multi-view renderings of database models and annotates them with descriptive captions using an image captioning model. At inference, GroundedSAM detects the queried object in the input image, and multi-modal embeddings are computed for both the Region-of-Interest and the database captions. OSCAR employs a two-stage retrieval: text-based filtering using CLIP identifies candidate models, followed by image-based refinement using DINOv2 to select the most visually similar object. In our experiments we demonstrate that OSCAR outperforms all state-of-the-art methods on the cross-domain 3D model retrieval benchmark MI3DOR. Furthermore, we demonstrate OSCAR's direct applicability in automating object model sourcing for 6D object pose estimation. We propose using the most similar object model for pose estimation if the exact instance is not available and show that OSCAR achieves an average precision of 90.48\% during object retrieval on the YCB-V object dataset. Moreover, we demonstrate that the most similar object model can be utilized for pose estimation using Megapose achieving better results than a reconstruction-based approach.
tldr: The paper introduces OSCAR, a training-free method for retrieving CAD models from a database using a language prompt and a single image, demonstrating its effectiveness in 3D model retrieval and application in 6D object pose estimation.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了OSCAR，一种无需训练的方法，通过语言提示和单张图像从数据库中检索CAD模型，展示了其在3D模型检索和6D物体姿态估计中的有效性。
summary_zh: 6D物体姿态估计在场景理解中发挥着关键作用，应用于机器人技术和增强现实等领域。为了支持此类场景中不断变化的物体集合的需求，现代零样本物体姿态估计器被开发出来，它们不需要特定于物体的训练，而仅依赖于CAD模型。一旦部署，这些模型很难获取，并且不断变化和增长的物体集合使得可靠地识别感兴趣的实例模型变得更加困难。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种从语言提示和单个图像进行开放集CAD检索的方法 (OSCAR)，这是一种新颖的、无需训练的方法，可以从一个未标记的3D物体数据库中检索匹配的物体模型。在初始阶段，OSCAR生成数据库模型的多视角渲染图，并使用图像字幕模型对其进行描述性标注。在推理阶段，GroundedSAM检测输入图像中查询的物体，并计算感兴趣区域和数据库标题的多模态嵌入。OSCAR采用两阶段检索：使用CLIP的基于文本的过滤识别候选模型，然后使用DINOv2的基于图像的细化选择视觉上最相似的物体。在我们的实验中，我们证明OSCAR在跨域3D模型检索基准MI3DOR上优于所有最先进的方法。此外，我们还展示了OSCAR在自动化物体模型采购方面对6D物体姿态估计的直接适用性。我们建议在无法获得确切实例的情况下，使用最相似的物体模型进行姿态估计，并表明OSCAR在YCB-V物体数据集上的物体检索中实现了90.48\%的平均精度。此外，我们证明了最相似的物体模型可以用于使用Megapose进行姿态估计，从而获得比基于重建的方法更好的结果。

title: Heterogeneous Multi-Expert Reinforcement Learning for Long-Horizon Multi-Goal Tasks in Autonomous Forklifts
summary: Autonomous mobile manipulation in unstructured warehouses requires a balance between efficient large-scale navigation and high-precision object interaction. Traditional end-to-end learning approaches often struggle to handle the conflicting demands of these distinct phases. Navigation relies on robust decision-making over large spaces, while manipulation needs high sensitivity to fine local details. Forcing a single network to learn these different objectives simultaneously often causes optimization interference, where improving one task degrades the other. To address these limitations, we propose a Heterogeneous Multi-Expert Reinforcement Learning (HMER) framework tailored for autonomous forklifts. HMER decomposes long-horizon tasks into specialized sub-policies controlled by a Semantic Task Planner. This structure separates macro-level navigation from micro-level manipulation, allowing each expert to focus on its specific action space without interference. The planner coordinates the sequential execution of these experts, bridging the gap between task planning and continuous control. Furthermore, to solve the problem of sparse exploration, we introduce a Hybrid Imitation-Reinforcement Training Strategy. This method uses expert demonstrations to initialize the policy and Reinforcement Learning for fine-tuning. Experiments in Gazebo simulations show that HMER significantly outperforms sequential and end-to-end baselines. Our method achieves a task success rate of 94.2\% (compared to 62.5\% for baselines), reduces operation time by 21.4\%, and maintains placement error within 1.5 cm, validating its efficacy for precise material handling.
tldr: The paper proposes a Heterogeneous Multi-Expert Reinforcement Learning (HMER) framework for autonomous forklift tasks, using a semantic task planner to coordinate specialized sub-policies for navigation and manipulation and a hybrid imitation-reinforcement training strategy to improve exploration and performance.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种用于自主叉车任务的异构多专家强化学习（HMER）框架，该框架使用语义任务规划器来协调用于navigation和manipulation的专用子策略，并采用混合模仿-强化学习训练策略来提高探索和性能。
summary_zh: 在非结构化仓库中的自主移动操作需要在高效的大规模导航和高精度物体交互之间取得平衡。传统的端到端学习方法通常难以应对这些不同阶段的冲突需求。导航依赖于在广阔空间上的稳健决策，而操作则需要对精细的局部细节具有高度敏感性。强迫单个网络同时学习这些不同的目标通常会导致优化干扰，即改进一项任务会降低另一项任务的性能。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了一种针对自主叉车的异构多专家强化学习（HMER）框架。HMER将长时域任务分解为由语义任务规划器控制的专门化的子策略。这种结构将宏观层面的导航与微观层面的操作分离，使每个专家能够专注于其特定的动作空间而不会产生干扰。规划器协调这些专家的顺序执行，弥合了任务规划和连续控制之间的差距。此外，为了解决稀疏探索问题，我们引入了一种混合模仿-强化训练策略。该方法使用专家演示来初始化策略，并使用强化学习进行微调。Gazebo仿真中的实验表明，HMER显著优于顺序和端到端基线。我们的方法实现了94.2%的任务成功率（相比于基线的62.5%），减少了21.4%的操作时间，并将放置误差保持在1.5厘米以内，验证了其在精确物料搬运方面的有效性。

title: AdaMorph: Unified Motion Retargeting via Embodiment-Aware Adaptive Transformers
summary: Retargeting human motion to heterogeneous robots is a fundamental challenge in robotics, primarily due to the severe kinematic and dynamic discrepancies between varying embodiments. Existing solutions typically resort to training embodiment-specific models, which scales poorly and fails to exploit shared motion semantics. To address this, we present AdaMorph, a unified neural retargeting framework that enables a single model to adapt human motion to diverse robot morphologies. Our approach treats retargeting as a conditional generation task. We map human motion into a morphology-agnostic latent intent space and utilize a dual-purpose prompting mechanism to condition the generation. Instead of simple input concatenation, we leverage Adaptive Layer Normalization (AdaLN) to dynamically modulate the decoder's feature space based on embodiment constraints. Furthermore, we enforce physical plausibility through a curriculum-based training objective that ensures orientation and trajectory consistency via integration. Experimental results on 12 distinct humanoid robots demonstrate that AdaMorph effectively unifies control across heterogeneous topologies, exhibiting strong zero-shot generalization to unseen complex motions while preserving the dynamic essence of the source behaviors.
tldr: AdaMorph presents a unified neural framework for retargeting human motion to diverse robots, using adaptive transformers and a novel prompting mechanism to achieve zero-shot generalization across varying morphologies.
tldr_zh: AdaMorph 提出了一个统一的神经框架，用于将人类运动重新定向到不同的机器人，使用自适应变换器和一种新颖的提示机制，以在不同的形态中实现零样本泛化。
summary_zh: 将人类运动重定向到异构机器人是机器人领域的一项根本性挑战，这主要是由于不同实体之间严重的运动学和动力学差异。现有的解决方案通常依赖于训练特定于实体的模型，这种方法扩展性较差且无法利用共享的运动语义。为了解决这个问题，我们提出AdaMorph，这是一个统一的神经重定向框架，能够使单个模型将人类运动适应于不同的机器人形态。我们的方法将重定向视为一个条件生成任务。我们将人类运动映射到一个与形态无关的潜在意图空间，并利用一个双重用途的提示机制来调节生成过程。我们没有采用简单的输入拼接，而是利用自适应层归一化（AdaLN）来基于实体约束动态地调节解码器的特征空间。此外，我们通过基于课程的学习目标来强制物理可行性，该目标通过积分确保姿态和轨迹的一致性。在12种不同的人形机器人上的实验结果表明，AdaMorph有效地统一了异构拓扑结构之间的控制，在保留源行为的动态本质的同时，对未见过的复杂运动表现出强大的零样本泛化能力。

title: PROTEA: Securing Robot Task Planning and Execution
summary: Robots need task planning methods to generate action sequences for complex tasks. Recent work on adversarial attacks has revealed significant vulnerabilities in existing robot task planners, especially those built on foundation models. In this paper, we aim to address these security challenges by introducing PROTEA, an LLM-as-a-Judge defense mechanism, to evaluate the security of task plans. PROTEA is developed to address the dimensionality and history challenges in plan safety assessment. We used different LLMs to implement multiple versions of PROTEA for comparison purposes. For systemic evaluations, we created a dataset containing both benign and malicious task plans, where the harmful behaviors were injected at varying levels of stealthiness. Our results provide actionable insights for robotic system practitioners seeking to enhance robustness and security of their task planning systems. Details, dataset and demos are provided: https://protea-secure.github.io/PROTEA/
tldr: The paper proposes PROTEA, an LLM-as-a-Judge defense mechanism, to evaluate and improve the security of task plans generated by robot task planning systems, especially those built on foundation models. It provides a dataset for systemic evaluation and insights for practitioners.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出PROTEA，一种基于LLM的打分防御机制，用于评估和提高机器人任务规划系统生成的任务计划的安全性，特别是那些基于基础模型的系统。它提供了一个数据集来进行系统评估，并为从业者提供见解。
summary_zh: 机器人需要任务规划方法来为复杂任务生成动作序列。最近关于对抗攻击的研究揭示了现有机器人任务规划器的显著漏洞，尤其是那些构建在基础模型之上的规划器。在本文中，我们旨在通过引入PROTEA，一种基于LLM的判别防御机制，来评估任务计划的安全性，从而解决这些安全挑战。开发PROTEA是为了解决计划安全评估中的维度和历史挑战。我们使用不同的LLM来实现多个版本的PROTEA，以进行比较。为了进行系统性评估，我们创建了一个包含良性和恶意任务计划的数据集，其中有害行为以不同程度的隐蔽性注入。我们的结果为希望提高其任务规划系统稳健性和安全性的机器人系统从业者提供了可操作的见解。详细信息、数据集和演示可在以下网址找到：https://protea-secure.github.io/PROTEA/

title: Beyond Single-Shot: Multi-step Tool Retrieval via Query Planning
summary: LLM agents operating over massive, dynamic tool libraries rely on effective retrieval, yet standard single-shot dense retrievers struggle with complex requests. These failures primarily stem from the disconnect between abstract user goals and technical documentation, and the limited capacity of fixed-size embeddings to model combinatorial tool compositions. To address these challenges, we propose TOOLQP, a lightweight framework that models retrieval as iterative query planning. Instead of single-shot matching, TOOLQP decomposes instructions into sub-tasks and dynamically generates queries to interact with the retriever, effectively bridging the semantic gap by targeting the specific sub-tasks required for composition. We train TOOLQP using synthetic query trajectories followed by optimization via Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR). Experiments demonstrate that TOOLQP achieves state-of-the-art performance, exhibiting superior zero-shot generalization, robustness across diverse retrievers, and significant improvements in downstream agentic execution.
tldr: The paper introduces TOOLQP, a framework for multi-step tool retrieval via query planning, addressing the limitations of single-shot retrievers in complex LLM agent tasks. It uses iterative query decomposition and Reinforcement Learning to improve retrieval accuracy and downstream agent execution.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为TOOLQP的框架，用于通过查询规划进行多步骤工具检索，解决了在复杂LLM代理任务中单次检索器的局限性。它使用迭代查询分解和强化学习来提高检索精度和下游代理执行。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型智能体依赖于庞大且动态的工具库运作，需要高效的检索，然而，标准的单次密集检索器在复杂请求面前表现不佳。这些失败主要源于抽象用户目标与技术文档之间的脱节，以及固定大小的嵌入对组合工具的建模能力有限。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出TOOLQP，一个将检索建模为迭代查询规划的轻量级框架。TOOLQP不采用单次匹配，而是将指令分解为子任务，并动态生成查询与检索器交互，通过针对组合所需的特定子任务，有效地弥合了语义鸿沟。我们使用合成查询轨迹训练TOOLQP，然后通过具有可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）进行优化。实验表明，TOOLQP取得了最先进的性能，展现出卓越的零样本泛化能力、对多种检索器的鲁棒性，以及下游智能体执行中的显著改进。

title: OS-Symphony: A Holistic Framework for Robust and Generalist Computer-Using Agent
summary: While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have significantly advanced Computer-Using Agents (CUAs), current frameworks struggle with robustness in long-horizon workflows and generalization in novel domains. These limitations stem from a lack of granular control over historical visual context curation and the absence of visual-aware tutorial retrieval. To bridge these gaps, we introduce OS-Symphony, a holistic framework that comprises an Orchestrator coordinating two key innovations for robust automation: (1) a Reflection-Memory Agent that utilizes milestone-driven long-term memory to enable trajectory-level self-correction, effectively mitigating visual context loss in long-horizon tasks; (2) Versatile Tool Agents featuring a Multimodal Searcher that adopts a SeeAct paradigm to navigate a browser-based sandbox to synthesize live, visually aligned tutorials, thereby resolving fidelity issues in unseen scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that OS-Symphony delivers substantial performance gains across varying model scales, establishing new state-of-the-art results on three online benchmarks, notably achieving 65.84% on OSWorld.
tldr: OS-Symphony is a new framework for Computer-Using Agents (CUAs) that uses milestone-driven long-term memory and multimodal search to improve robustness and generalization in long-horizon tasks and unseen scenarios, achieving state-of-the-art results on online benchmarks.
tldr_zh: OS-Symphony是一个新的计算机使用代理框架，它使用里程碑驱动的长期记忆和多模式搜索来提高在长时程任务和未见场景中的鲁棒性和泛化能力，并在在线基准测试中实现了最新的结果。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言模型（VLMs）已显著推进了计算机辅助智能体（CUAs）的发展，但当前的框架在长程工作流的鲁棒性和新领域的泛化方面仍存在不足。这些局限性源于对历史视觉上下文管理缺乏精细控制，以及缺乏视觉感知的教程检索。为了弥合这些差距，我们提出了OS-Symphony，一个整体框架，包含一个协调器，它协调两个关键创新技术以实现稳健的自动化：（1）一个反思-记忆智能体，它利用里程碑驱动的长期记忆来实现轨迹层面的自我纠正，有效地减轻了长程任务中的视觉上下文丢失；（2）具有多功能工具的智能体，该智能体配备了一个多模态搜索器，它采用SeeAct范式来导航基于浏览器的沙盒，从而合成实时的、视觉对齐的教程，进而解决了在未见场景中的保真度问题。实验结果表明，OS-Symphony在不同的模型规模上均实现了显著的性能提升，在三个在线基准测试上确立了新的最先进水平，尤其是在OSWorld上达到了65.84%。

title: Beyond Static Tools: Test-Time Tool Evolution for Scientific Reasoning
summary: The central challenge of AI for Science is not reasoning alone, but the ability to create computational methods in an open-ended scientific world. Existing LLM-based agents rely on static, pre-defined tool libraries, a paradigm that fundamentally fails in scientific domains where tools are sparse, heterogeneous, and intrinsically incomplete. In this paper, we propose Test-Time Tool Evolution (TTE), a new paradigm that enables agents to synthesize, verify, and evolve executable tools during inference. By transforming tools from fixed resources into problem-driven artifacts, TTE overcomes the rigidity and long-tail limitations of static tool libraries. To facilitate rigorous evaluation, we introduce SciEvo, a benchmark comprising 1,590 scientific reasoning tasks supported by 925 automatically evolved tools. Extensive experiments show that TTE achieves state-of-the-art performance in both accuracy and tool efficiency, while enabling effective cross-domain adaptation of computational tools. The code and benchmark have been released at https://github.com/lujiaxuan0520/Test-Time-Tool-Evol.
tldr: The paper introduces Test-Time Tool Evolution (TTE), a method for LLM-based agents to dynamically create and evolve their own tools during inference for scientific reasoning, and presents SciEvo, a benchmark for evaluating this approach.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了测试时工具演化 (TTE)，这是一种使基于 LLM 的智能体能够在推理过程中动态创建和演化其自身工具的方法，用于科学推理，并提出了一个名为 SciEvo 的基准来评估这种方法。
summary_zh: 人工智能赋能科学的核心挑战并非仅仅是推理，而是要在开放式的科学世界中创造计算方法的能力。现有基于大语言模型的智能代理依赖于静态、预定义的工具库，这种范式在工具稀疏、异构且本质上不完整的科学领域中根本无法奏效。在本文中，我们提出了测试时工具演化（TTE），一种使智能代理能够在推理过程中合成、验证和演化可执行工具的新范式。通过将工具从固定的资源转化为问题驱动的人工制品，TTE克服了静态工具库的刚性和长尾局限性。为了便于严格评估，我们引入了SciEvo，一个包含1590个科学推理任务的基准，并由925个自动演化的工具提供支持。大量的实验表明，TTE在准确性和工具效率方面都达到了最先进的性能，同时实现了计算工具有效的跨领域适应。代码和基准已在https://github.com/lujiaxuan0520/Test-Time-Tool-Evol发布。

title: GeoMotionGPT: Geometry-Aligned Motion Understanding with Large Language Models
summary: Discrete motion tokenization has recently enabled Large Language Models (LLMs) to serve as versatile backbones for motion understanding and motion-language reasoning. However, existing pipelines typically decouple motion quantization from semantic embedding learning, linking them solely via token IDs. This approach fails to effectively align the intrinsic geometry of the motion space with the embedding space, thereby hindering the LLM's capacity for nuanced motion reasoning. We argue that alignment is most effective when both modalities share a unified geometric basis. Therefore, instead of forcing the LLM to reconstruct the complex geometry among motion tokens from scratch, we present a novel framework that explicitly enforces orthogonality on both the motion codebook and the LLM embedding space, ensuring that their relational structures naturally mirror each other. Specifically, we employ a decoder-only quantizer with Gumbel-Softmax for differentiable training and balanced codebook usage. To bridge the modalities, we use a sparse projection that maps motion codes into the LLM embedding space while preserving orthogonality. Finally, a two-stage orthonormal regularization schedule enforces soft constraints during tokenizer training and LLM fine-tuning to maintain geometric alignment without hindering semantic adaptation. Extensive experiments on HumanML3D demonstrate that our framework achieves a 20% performance improvement over current state-of-the-art methods, validating that a unified geometric basis effectively empowers the LLM for nuanced motion reasoning.
tldr: GeoMotionGPT aligns the geometry of motion tokenization with LLM embedding space via orthogonality constraints, achieving improved performance in motion-language reasoning tasks. This allows LLMs to better understand and reason about human motion.
tldr_zh: GeoMotionGPT通过正交约束将运动标记化的几何结构与LLM嵌入空间对齐，从而在运动-语言推理任务中实现了更好的性能。这使得LLM能够更好地理解和推理人体运动。
summary_zh: 离散运动令牌化最近使得大型语言模型（LLMs）能够作为通用的骨干网络，用于运动理解和运动-语言推理。然而，现有的流程通常将运动量化与语义嵌入学习解耦，仅通过令牌ID将它们连接起来。这种方法不能有效地将运动空间的内在几何结构与嵌入空间对齐，从而阻碍了LLM进行细致运动推理的能力。我们认为，当两种模态共享一个统一的几何基础时，对齐是最有效的。因此，我们提出了一种新型框架，该框架显式地在运动码本和LLM嵌入空间上强制执行正交性，确保它们的关系结构自然地相互映射，而不是强迫LLM从头开始重建运动令牌之间复杂的几何结构。具体而言，我们采用带有Gumbel-Softmax的仅解码器量化器进行可微训练和平衡的码本使用。为了桥接模态，我们使用一个稀疏投影，将运动代码映射到LLM嵌入空间，同时保持正交性。最后，一个两阶段正交正则化策略在令牌器训练和LLM微调期间施加软约束，以保持几何对齐，而不妨碍语义适应。在HumanML3D上的大量实验表明，我们的框架比当前最先进的方法实现了20%的性能提升，验证了统一的几何基础有效地增强了LLM进行细致运动推理的能力。

title: Large Language Models for Physics Instrument Design
summary: We study the use of large language models (LLMs) for physics instrument design and compare their performance to reinforcement learning (RL). Using only prompting, LLMs are given task constraints and summaries of prior high-scoring designs and propose complete detector configurations, which we evaluate with the same simulators and reward functions used in RL-based optimization. Although RL yields stronger final designs, we find that modern LLMs consistently generate valid, resource-aware, and physically meaningful configurations that draw on broad pretrained knowledge of detector design principles and particle--matter interactions, despite having no task-specific training. Based on this result, as a first step toward hybrid design workflows, we explore pairing the LLMs with a dedicated trust region optimizer, serving as a precursor to future pipelines in which LLMs propose and structure design hypotheses while RL performs reward-driven optimization. Based on these experiments, we argue that LLMs are well suited as meta-planners: they can design and orchestrate RL-based optimization studies, define search strategies, and coordinate multiple interacting components within a unified workflow. In doing so, they point toward automated, closed-loop instrument design in which much of the human effort required to structure and supervise optimization can be reduced.
tldr: The paper explores the use of LLMs for physics instrument design, finding they can generate valid configurations and serve as meta-planners for RL-based optimization, suggesting a path towards automated, closed-loop instrument design.
tldr_zh: 该论文探讨了使用大型语言模型进行物理仪器设计，发现它们可以生成有效的配置，并作为基于强化学习的优化的元规划器，从而提出了自动化、闭环的仪器设计方法。
summary_zh: 我们研究了大型语言模型（LLMs）在物理仪器设计中的应用，并将它们的性能与强化学习（RL）进行比较。仅使用提示，我们为LLMs提供任务约束和先前高分设计的总结，并让其提出完整的探测器配置，我们使用与基于RL的优化中相同的模拟器和奖励函数来评估这些配置。虽然RL能产生更优的最终设计，但我们发现，现代LLMs能够始终如一地生成有效、资源敏感且具有物理意义的配置，这些配置汲取了探测器设计原则和粒子-物质相互作用的广泛预训练知识，尽管没有任何特定于任务的训练。基于这一结果，作为混合设计工作流程的第一步，我们探索将LLMs与专用信任域优化器配对，作为未来流水线的先导，在这些流水线中，LLMs提出和构建设计假设，而RL执行奖励驱动的优化。基于这些实验，我们认为LLMs非常适合作为元规划器：它们可以设计和组织基于RL的优化研究，定义搜索策略，并在统一的工作流程中协调多个相互作用的组件。通过这样做，它们指向自动化、闭环仪器设计，其中可以减少构建和监督优化所需的大部分人为工作。

title: Beyond Entangled Planning: Task-Decoupled Planning for Long-Horizon Agents
summary: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have enabled agents to autonomously execute complex, long-horizon tasks, yet planning remains a primary bottleneck for reliable task execution. Existing methods typically fall into two paradigms: step-wise planning, which is reactive but often short-sighted; and one-shot planning, which generates a complete plan upfront yet is brittle to execution errors. Crucially, both paradigms suffer from entangled contexts, where the agent must reason over a monolithic history spanning multiple sub-tasks. This entanglement increases cognitive load and lets local errors propagate across otherwise independent decisions, making recovery computationally expensive. To address this, we propose Task-Decoupled Planning (TDP), a training-free framework that replaces entangled reasoning with task decoupling. TDP decomposes tasks into a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of sub-goals via a Supervisor. Using a Planner and Executor with scoped contexts, TDP confines reasoning and replanning to the active sub-task. This isolation prevents error propagation and corrects deviations locally without disrupting the workflow. Results on TravelPlanner, ScienceWorld, and HotpotQA show that TDP outperforms strong baselines while reducing token consumption by up to 82%, demonstrating that sub-task decoupling improves both robustness and efficiency for long-horizon agents.
tldr: The paper introduces Task-Decoupled Planning (TDP), a novel framework that decomposes long-horizon tasks into sub-goals, improving robustness and efficiency by preventing error propagation compared to existing planning paradigms with entangled contexts.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为任务解耦规划 (TDP) 的新框架，它将长期的任务分解为子目标，通过防止错误传播，与现有的具有纠缠上下文的规划范式相比，提高了鲁棒性和效率。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的最新进展已经使智能体能够自主执行复杂的、长时程任务，然而，规划仍然是可靠任务执行的主要瓶颈。现有方法通常分为两种范式：步进式规划，它具有反应性但往往目光短浅；以及一次性规划，它预先生成完整的计划，但易受执行错误的影响。关键的是，这两种范式都存在上下文纠缠的问题，智能体必须对跨越多个子任务的单一历史进行推理。这种纠缠增加了认知负荷，并使局部错误在原本独立的决策中传播，从而导致恢复的计算成本很高。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了任务解耦规划（TDP），这是一个无需训练的框架，它用任务解耦取代了上下文纠缠的推理。TDP通过一个监督器将任务分解为子目标的有向无环图（DAG）。使用具有限定上下文的规划器和执行器，TDP将推理和重新规划限制在活跃的子任务中。这种隔离防止了错误传播，并在不中断工作流程的情况下局部地纠正偏差。在TravelPlanner、ScienceWorld和HotpotQA上的结果表明，TDP优于强大的基线，同时减少了高达82%的token消耗，证明了子任务解耦提高了长时程智能体的鲁棒性和效率。

title: VirtualEnv: A Platform for Embodied AI Research
summary: As large language models (LLMs) continue to improve in reasoning and decision-making, there is a growing need for realistic and interactive environments where their abilities can be rigorously evaluated. We present VirtualEnv, a next-generation simulation platform built on Unreal Engine 5 that enables fine-grained benchmarking of LLMs in embodied and interactive scenarios. VirtualEnv supports rich agent-environment interactions, including object manipulation, navigation, and adaptive multi-agent collaboration, as well as game-inspired mechanics like escape rooms and procedurally generated environments. We provide a user-friendly API built on top of Unreal Engine, allowing researchers to deploy and control LLM-driven agents using natural language instructions. We integrate large-scale LLMs and vision-language models (VLMs), such as GPT-based models, to generate novel environments and structured tasks from multimodal inputs. Our experiments benchmark the performance of several popular LLMs across tasks of increasing complexity, analyzing differences in adaptability, planning, and multi-agent coordination. We also describe our methodology for procedural task generation, task validation, and real-time environment control. VirtualEnv is released as an open-source platform, we aim to advance research at the intersection of AI and gaming, enable standardized evaluation of LLMs in embodied AI settings, and pave the way for future developments in immersive simulations and interactive entertainment.
tldr: The paper introduces VirtualEnv, an open-source simulation platform built on Unreal Engine 5 for benchmarking LLMs in embodied AI scenarios, featuring rich agent-environment interactions and procedural task generation.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个名为VirtualEnv的开源模拟平台，该平台基于虚幻引擎5构建，用于在具身AI场景中对LLM进行基准测试，具有丰富的代理-环境交互和程序化任务生成。
summary_zh: 随着大型语言模型（LLM）在推理和决策方面的持续改进，越来越需要逼真和交互式的环境来严格评估它们的能力。我们提出了VirtualEnv，一个构建于虚幻引擎5之上的下一代仿真平台，它能够在具身和交互式场景中对LLM进行细粒度的基准测试。VirtualEnv支持丰富的智能体-环境交互，包括物体操作、导航和自适应的多智能体协作，以及密室逃脱和程序化生成环境等游戏启发的机制。我们提供了一个构建于虚幻引擎之上的用户友好型API，允许研究人员使用自然语言指令部署和控制LLM驱动的智能体。我们集成了大规模LLM和视觉-语言模型（VLM），例如基于GPT的模型，以从多模态输入中生成新的环境和结构化任务。我们的实验对几种流行的LLM在复杂度递增的任务中的性能进行了基准测试，分析了它们在适应性、规划和多智能体协调方面的差异。我们还描述了我们的程序化任务生成、任务验证和实时环境控制的方法。VirtualEnv作为一个开源平台发布，我们的目标是推进人工智能和游戏交叉领域的研究，实现具身人工智能环境中LLM的标准化评估，并为沉浸式模拟和交互式娱乐的未来发展铺平道路。

title: Controlling Multimodal Conversational Agents with Coverage-Enhanced Latent Actions
summary: Vision-language models are increasingly employed as multimodal conversational agents (MCAs) for diverse conversational tasks. Recently, reinforcement learning (RL) has been widely explored for adapting MCAs to various human-AI interaction scenarios. Despite showing great enhancement in generalization performance, fine-tuning MCAs via RL still faces challenges in handling the extremely large text token space. To address this, we learn a compact latent action space for RL fine-tuning instead. Specifically, we adopt the learning from observation mechanism to construct the codebook for the latent action space, where future observations are leveraged to estimate current latent actions that could further be used to reconstruct future observations. However, the scarcity of paired image-text data hinders learning a codebook with sufficient coverage. Thus, we leverage both paired image-text data and text-only data to construct the latent action space, using a cross-modal projector for transforming text embeddings into image-text embeddings. We initialize the cross-modal projector on paired image-text data, and further train it on massive text-only data with a novel cycle consistency loss to enhance its robustness. We show that our latent action based method outperforms competitive baselines on two conversation tasks across various RL algorithms.
tldr: This paper presents a method for fine-tuning multimodal conversational agents (MCAs) using reinforcement learning by learning a compact, coverage-enhanced latent action space to address the large text token space challenge. They leverage both paired image-text data and text-only data with a novel cycle consistency loss to improve the codebook.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种使用强化学习微调多模态对话代理（MCA）的方法，通过学习一个紧凑的，覆盖增强的潜在动作空间来解决大型文本token空间的挑战。他们利用配对的图像-文本数据和仅文本数据，并使用一种新的循环一致性损失来改进代码本。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言模型正日益被用作多模态对话代理（MCAs），以执行各种对话任务。 近期，强化学习（RL）被广泛应用于调整MCAs以适应各种人机交互情境。 尽管在泛化性能上表现出显著提升，但通过RL微调MCAs在处理极其庞大的文本token空间时仍然面临挑战。 为了解决这个问题，我们转而学习一个紧凑的潜在动作空间用于RL微调。 具体而言，我们采用从观察中学习机制来构建潜在动作空间的码本，其中利用未来的观察来估计当前的潜在动作，而该潜在动作可被进一步用于重建未来的观察。 然而，配对图像-文本数据的稀缺性阻碍了构建具有足够覆盖度的码本。 因此，我们同时利用配对图像-文本数据和纯文本数据来构建潜在动作空间，并使用跨模态投影器将文本嵌入转换成图像-文本嵌入。 我们在配对图像-文本数据上初始化跨模态投影器，并使用一种新颖的循环一致性损失在海量纯文本数据上进一步训练它，以增强其鲁棒性。 我们证明了我们基于潜在动作的方法在各种RL算法下，于两个对话任务中均优于有竞争力的基线方法。

title: Learning How to Remember: A Meta-Cognitive Management Method for Structured and Transferable Agent Memory
summary: Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on accumulated memory to solve long-horizon decision-making tasks. However, most existing approaches store memory in fixed representations and reuse it at a single or implicit level of abstraction, which limits generalization and often leads to negative transfer when distribution shift. This paper proposes the Meta-Cognitive Memory Abstraction method (MCMA), which treats memory abstraction as a learnable cognitive skill rather than a fixed design choice. MCMA decouples task execution from memory management by combining a frozen task model with a learned memory copilot. The memory copilot is trained using direct preference optimization, it determines how memories should be structured, abstracted, and reused. Memories are further organized into a hierarchy of abstraction levels, enabling selective reuse based on task similarity. When no memory is transferable, MCMA transfers the ability to abstract and manage memory by transferring the memory copilot. Experiments on ALFWorld, ScienceWorld, and BabyAI demonstrate substantial improvements in performance, out-of-distribution generalization, and cross-task transfer over several baselines.
tldr: The paper introduces Meta-Cognitive Memory Abstraction (MCMA), a method that learns how to structure, abstract, and reuse memories for LLM agents, improving performance and transferability in long-horizon decision-making tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了元认知记忆抽象 (MCMA) 方法，该方法学习如何构建、抽象和重用 LLM 智能体的记忆，从而提高在长程决策任务中的性能和可迁移性。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型(LLM)智能体越来越依赖于积累的记忆来解决长程决策任务。然而，现有的大多数方法都将记忆存储在固定的表征中，并在单一或隐式的抽象层级上复用它，这限制了泛化能力，并且在分布偏移时经常导致负迁移。本文提出了一种元认知记忆抽象方法(MCMA)，该方法将记忆抽象视为一种可学习的认知技能，而非固定的设计选择。MCMA通过将冻结的任务模型与学习到的记忆副驾驶相结合，将任务执行与记忆管理解耦。记忆副驾驶使用直接偏好优化进行训练，它决定了记忆应该如何被构建、抽象和复用。记忆被进一步组织成一个抽象层级结构，从而能够基于任务相似性进行选择性复用。当没有记忆可以迁移时，MCMA会通过迁移记忆副驾驶来迁移抽象和管理记忆的能力。在ALFWorld、ScienceWorld和BabyAI上的实验表明，相对于几个基线，MCMA在性能、分布外泛化和跨任务迁移方面都有显著改进。

title: Knowledge Distillation for LLM-Based Human Activity Recognition in Homes
summary: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) is a central problem for context-aware applications, especially for smart homes and assisted living. A few very recent studies have shown that Large Language Models (LLMs) can be used for HAR at home, reaching high performance and addressing key challenges. In this paper, we provide new experimental results regarding the use of LLMs for HAR, on two state-of-the-art datasets. More specifically, we show how recognition performance evolves depending on the size of the LLM used. Moreover, we experiment on the use of knowledge distillation techniques to fine-tune smaller LLMs with HAR reasoning examples generated by larger LLMs. We show that such fine-tuned models can perform almost as well as the largest LLMs, while having 50 times less parameters.
tldr: This paper explores using knowledge distillation to fine-tune smaller LLMs for Human Activity Recognition (HAR) in smart homes, achieving performance comparable to larger LLMs with significantly fewer parameters.
tldr_zh: 本文探讨了使用知识蒸馏来微调较小的LLM，用于智能家居中的人类活动识别（HAR）。结果表明，这种方法可以用明显更少的参数获得与大型LLM相当的性能。
summary_zh: 人体活动识别（HAR）是情境感知应用的核心问题，尤其对于智能家居和辅助生活而言。最近的一些研究表明，大型语言模型（LLM）可用于家庭环境中的HAR，并达到高性能，同时解决关键挑战。在本文中，我们提供了关于使用LLM进行HAR的最新实验结果，基于两个最先进的数据集。更具体地说，我们展示了识别性能如何随着所使用LLM规模的变化而演变。此外，我们实验了使用知识蒸馏技术，利用由较大LLM生成的HAR推理示例来微调较小LLM。我们表明，这种微调后的模型能够达到几乎与最大LLM相当的性能，同时参数量减少了50倍。

title: Puzzle it Out: Local-to-Global World Model for Offline Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
summary: Offline multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) aims to solve cooperative decision-making problems in multi-agent systems using pre-collected datasets. Existing offline MARL methods primarily constrain training within the dataset distribution, resulting in overly conservative policies that struggle to generalize beyond the support of the data. While model-based approaches offer a promising solution by expanding the original dataset with synthetic data generated from a learned world model, the high dimensionality, non-stationarity, and complexity of multi-agent systems make it challenging to accurately estimate the transitions and reward functions in offline MARL. Given the difficulty of directly modeling joint dynamics, we propose a local-to-global (LOGO) world model, a novel framework that leverages local predictions-which are easier to estimate-to infer global state dynamics, thus improving prediction accuracy while implicitly capturing agent-wise dependencies. Using the trained world model, we generate synthetic data to augment the original dataset, expanding the effective state-action space. To ensure reliable policy learning, we further introduce an uncertainty-aware sampling mechanism that adaptively weights synthetic data by prediction uncertainty, reducing approximation error propagation to policies. In contrast to conventional ensemble-based methods, our approach requires only an additional encoder for uncertainty estimation, significantly reducing computational overhead while maintaining accuracy. Extensive experiments across 8 scenarios against 8 baselines demonstrate that our method surpasses state-of-the-art baselines on standard offline MARL benchmarks, establishing a new model-based baseline for generalizable offline multi-agent learning.
tldr: This paper introduces a local-to-global world model (LOGO) for offline multi-agent reinforcement learning, using local predictions to infer global dynamics and an uncertainty-aware sampling mechanism to improve policy learning with synthetic data.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种用于离线多智能体强化学习的局部到全局世界模型 (LOGO)，该模型利用局部预测来推断全局动态，并使用一种不确定性感知采样机制来改进使用合成数据的策略学习。
summary_zh: 离线多智能体强化学习（MARL）旨在利用预先收集的数据集解决多智能体系统中的协作决策问题。现有的离线MARL方法主要将训练限制在数据集分布内，导致过度保守的策略，难以在数据支持范围之外进行泛化。虽然基于模型的方法通过使用从学习的世界模型生成的合成数据来扩展原始数据集提供了一个有希望的解决方案，但多智能体系统的高维度、非平稳性和复杂性使得在离线MARL中准确估计转移和奖励函数具有挑战性。鉴于直接建模联合动态的困难，我们提出了一种局部到全局（LOGO）世界模型，这是一个新颖的框架，它利用局部预测（更容易估计）来推断全局状态动态，从而提高预测准确性，同时隐式地捕获智能体之间的依赖关系。利用训练好的世界模型，我们生成合成数据来扩充原始数据集，从而扩展有效的状态-动作空间。为了确保可靠的策略学习，我们进一步引入了一种不确定性感知采样机制，该机制通过预测不确定性自适应地对合成数据进行加权，从而减少近似误差向策略的传播。与传统的基于集成的方法相比，我们的方法只需要一个额外的编码器进行不确定性估计，从而在保持准确性的同时显著降低计算开销。在8个场景中针对8个基线的广泛实验表明，我们的方法在标准离线MARL基准测试中超越了最先进的基线，为可泛化的离线多智能体学习建立了一个新的基于模型的基线。

title: OpenTinker: Separating Concerns in Agentic Reinforcement Learning
summary: We introduce OpenTinker, an infrastructure for reinforcement learning (RL) of large language model (LLM) agents built around a separation of concerns across algorithm design, execution, and agent-environment interaction. Rather than relying on monolithic, end-to-end RL pipelines, OpenTinker decomposes agentic learning systems into lightweight, composable components with clearly defined abstraction boundaries. Users specify agents, environments, and interaction protocols, while inference and training are delegated to a managed execution runtime. OpenTinker introduces a centralized scheduler for managing training and inference workloads, including LoRA-based and full-parameter RL, supervised fine-tuning, and inference, over shared resources. We further discuss design principles for extending OpenTinker to multi-agent training. Finally, we present a set of RL use cases that demonstrate the effectiveness of the framework in practical agentic learning scenarios.
tldr: OpenTinker is an infrastructure for agentic RL using LLMs that separates concerns into composable components, managed by a centralized scheduler to support various RL and fine-tuning approaches. It demonstrates effectiveness in practical agentic learning scenarios.
tldr_zh: OpenTinker是一个基于LLM的Agentic RL基础设施，它将关注点分离为可组合的组件，并由中央调度器管理，以支持各种RL和微调方法。它展示了该框架在实际agentic学习场景中的有效性。
summary_zh: 我们介绍了 OpenTinker，一个用于大型语言模型（LLM）智能体的强化学习（RL）基础设施，其构建围绕算法设计、执行和智能体-环境交互之间的关注点分离展开。与依赖于单一整体、端到端的强化学习流程不同，OpenTinker将智能体学习系统分解为轻量级、可组合的组件，并具有清晰的抽象边界。用户指定智能体、环境和交互协议，而推理和训练则委托给托管的执行运行时。OpenTinker 引入了一个集中式调度器，用于管理训练和推理工作负载，包括基于 LoRA 的和全参数强化学习、监督式微调以及在共享资源上的推理。我们进一步讨论了将 OpenTinker 扩展到多智能体训练的设计原则。最后，我们展示了一组强化学习用例，这些用例证明了该框架在实际智能体学习场景中的有效性。

title: Agentic Diagnostic Reasoning over Telecom and Datacenter Infrastructure
summary:
Large-scale telecom and datacenter infrastructures rely on multi-layered service and resource models, where failures propagate across physical and logical components and affect multiple customers. Traditional approaches to root cause analysis(RCA) rely on hard-coded graph traversal algorithms or rule-based correlation engines, which are costly to maintain and tightly coupled to the infrastructure model.
  In this work, we introduce an agentic diagnostic framework where a Large Language Model (LLM) performs step-wise investigation using a constrained tool space exposed through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Instead of embedding causal logic or traversal algorithms into the application, the agent autonomously navigates the infrastructure model by invoking tools for service lookup, dependency retrieval, structured and unstructured data, and event analysis, and impact discovery. We define an investigation protocol that structures the agent's reasoning and ensures grounding, reproducibility, and safe handling of missing or ambiguous information.
  This work lays the foundation for autonomous incident resolution and change impact mitigation. Future systems will not only diagnose and remediate infrastructure failures, but also predict the impact of planned changes on services and customers, enabling operators to mitigate risks before executing maintenance operations.
tldr: This paper introduces an agentic diagnostic framework using LLMs to autonomously navigate infrastructure models for root cause analysis in telecom and datacenter environments, promising improved incident resolution and change impact mitigation.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个基于LLM的Agentic诊断框架，通过自主导航基础设施模型来分析电信和数据中心环境中的根本原因，有望改善事件解决和变更影响缓解。
summary_zh:
大规模电信和数据中心基础设施依赖于多层服务和资源模型，其中故障在物理和逻辑组件间传播，并影响多个客户。传统的根因分析（RCA）方法依赖于硬编码的图遍历算法或基于规则的相关性引擎，这些方法维护成本高昂，并且与基础设施模型紧密耦合。

在这项工作中，我们引入了一个代理诊断框架，其中大型语言模型（LLM）使用通过模型上下文协议（MCP）暴露的受限工具空间执行逐步调查。该代理不是将因果逻辑或遍历算法嵌入到应用程序中，而是通过调用服务查找、依赖关系检索、结构化和非结构化数据、事件分析以及影响发现等工具自主地导航基础设施模型。我们定义了一种调查协议，该协议构建了代理的推理过程，并确保其扎根性、可重复性以及安全处理缺失或模糊信息。

这项工作为自主事件解决和变更影响缓解奠定了基础。未来的系统不仅将诊断和修复基础设施故障，还将预测计划变更对服务和客户的影响，使运营商能够在执行维护操作之前减轻风险。

title: ARM: Role-Conditioned Neuron Transplantation for Training-Free Generalist LLM Agent Merging
summary: Interactive large language model agents have advanced rapidly, but most remain specialized to a single environment and fail to adapt robustly to other environments. Model merging offers a training-free alternative by integrating multiple experts into a single model. In this paper, we propose Agent-Role Merging (ARM), an activation-guided, role-conditioned neuron transplantation method for model merging in LLM agents. ARM improves existing merging methods from static natural language tasks to multi-turn agent scenarios, and over the generalization ability across various interactive environments. This is achieved with a well designed 3-step framework: 1) constructing merged backbones, 2) selection based on its role-conditioned activation analysis, and 3) neuron transplantation for fine-grained refinements. Without gradient-based optimization, ARM improves cross-benchmark generalization while enjoying efficiency. Across diverse domains, the model obtained via ARM merging outperforms prior model merging methods and domain-specific expert models, while demonstrating strong out-of-domain generalization.
tldr: This paper introduces Agent-Role Merging (ARM), a training-free neuron transplantation method for merging LLM agents, improving generalization across diverse interactive environments without gradient-based optimization.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了 Agent-Role Merging (ARM)，一种无需训练的神经元移植方法，用于合并 LLM 智能体，从而提高在各种交互环境中的泛化能力，且无需基于梯度的优化。
summary_zh: 交互式大型语言模型代理发展迅速，但大多数仍然专注于单一环境，并且无法稳健地适应其他环境。模型融合提供了一种免训练的替代方案，通过将多个专家模型集成到一个模型中。在本文中，我们提出了一种代理角色融合（ARM）方法，这是一种激活引导的、角色条件神经元移植方法，用于LLM代理中的模型融合。ARM将现有的融合方法从静态自然语言任务改进到多轮代理场景，并提升了跨各种交互式环境的泛化能力。这得益于精心设计的 3 步框架：1) 构建融合骨干网络，2) 基于角色条件激活分析进行选择，以及 3) 神经元移植以进行细粒度优化。在没有基于梯度的优化的情况下，ARM 提高了跨基准测试的泛化能力，同时保持了效率。在不同的领域中，通过ARM融合得到的模型优于先前的模型融合方法和特定领域的专家模型，同时展示了强大的域外泛化能力。

title: Consolidation or Adaptation? PRISM: Disentangling SFT and RL Data via Gradient Concentration
summary: While Hybrid Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) followed by Reinforcement Learning (RL) has become the standard paradigm for training LLM agents, effective mechanisms for data allocation between these stages remain largely underexplored. Current data arbitration strategies often rely on surface-level heuristics that fail to diagnose intrinsic learning needs. Since SFT targets pattern consolidation through imitation while RL drives structural adaptation via exploration, misaligning data with these functional roles causes severe optimization interference. We propose PRISM, a dynamics-aware framework grounded in Schema Theory that arbitrates data based on its degree of cognitive conflict with the model's existing knowledge. By analyzing the spatial geometric structure of gradients, PRISM identifies data triggering high spatial concentration as high-conflict signals that require RL for structural restructuring. In contrast, data yielding diffuse updates is routed to SFT for efficient consolidation. Extensive experiments on WebShop and ALFWorld demonstrate that PRISM achieves a Pareto improvement, outperforming state-of-the-art hybrid methods while reducing computational costs by up to 3.22$\times$. Our findings suggest that disentangling data based on internal optimization regimes is crucial for scalable and robust agent alignment.
tldr: The paper introduces PRISM, a method that disentangles SFT and RL data based on gradient concentration to improve LLM agent training efficiency and performance by better aligning data with the appropriate training stage (SFT or RL).
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了PRISM，一种基于梯度浓度分离SFT和RL数据的方法，通过将数据与适当的训练阶段（SFT或RL）更好对齐，从而提高LLM代理训练的效率和性能。
summary_zh: 混合监督微调（SFT）后接强化学习（RL）已成为训练 LLM 代理的标准范式，但在这两个阶段之间有效分配数据的机制在很大程度上仍未得到探索。当前的数据仲裁策略通常依赖于表层启发式方法，无法诊断内在的学习需求。由于 SFT 通过模仿来巩固模式，而 RL 通过探索来驱动结构适应，因此将数据与这些功能角色错配会导致严重的优化干扰。我们提出了 PRISM，一个基于模式理论的、具有动态感知能力的框架，它根据数据与模型现有知识的认知冲突程度来仲裁数据。通过分析梯度的空间几何结构，PRISM 将触发高空间浓度的数据识别为高冲突信号，这些信号需要 RL 进行结构重塑。相反，产生扩散更新的数据将被路由到 SFT 以进行高效巩固。在 WebShop 和 ALFWorld 上的大量实验表明，PRISM 实现了帕累托改进，优于最先进的混合方法，同时将计算成本降低了高达 3.22 倍。我们的研究结果表明，基于内部优化机制来分离数据对于可扩展和稳健的代理对齐至关重要。

title: Active Context Compression: Autonomous Memory Management in LLM Agents
summary: Large Language Model (LLM) agents struggle with long-horizon software engineering tasks due to "Context Bloat." As interaction history grows, computational costs explode, latency increases, and reasoning capabilities degrade due to distraction by irrelevant past errors. Existing solutions often rely on passive, external summarization mechanisms that the agent cannot control. This paper proposes Focus, an agent-centric architecture inspired by the biological exploration strategies of Physarum polycephalum (slime mold). The Focus Agent autonomously decides when to consolidate key learnings into a persistent "Knowledge" block and actively withdraws (prunes) the raw interaction history. Using an optimized scaffold matching industry best practices (persistent bash + string-replacement editor), we evaluated Focus on N=5 context-intensive instances from SWE-bench Lite using Claude Haiku 4.5. With aggressive prompting that encourages frequent compression, Focus achieves 22.7% token reduction (14.9M -> 11.5M tokens) while maintaining identical accuracy (3/5 = 60% for both agents). Focus performed 6.0 autonomous compressions per task on average, with token savings up to 57% on individual instances. We demonstrate that capable models can autonomously self-regulate their context when given appropriate tools and prompting, opening pathways for cost-aware agentic systems without sacrificing task performance.
tldr: This paper introduces Focus, an LLM agent architecture inspired by slime mold, that autonomously compresses its context by consolidating learnings and pruning irrelevant history, achieving token reduction without sacrificing accuracy on software engineering tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为Focus的 LLM 代理架构，其灵感来自黏菌，通过整合学习和删除不相关的历史记录来自动压缩其上下文，从而在软件工程任务上实现token减少，同时不牺牲准确性。
summary_zh: 由于"上下文膨胀"，大型语言模型（LLM）智能体在处理长程软件工程任务时面临挑战。随着交互历史的增长，计算成本急剧增加，延迟升高，并且由于无关的过往错误分散注意力，推理能力下降。现有的解决方案通常依赖于被动的、外部的摘要机制，智能体无法控制。本文提出了一种名为Focus的、以智能体为中心的架构，其灵感来源于多头绒泡菌（Physarum polycephalum，俗称粘菌）的生物探索策略。Focus智能体自主决定何时将关键的学习经验整合到持久的“知识”块中，并主动撤回（剪枝）原始的交互历史。我们使用优化的、符合行业最佳实践的支架（持久的bash环境 + 字符串替换编辑器），利用Claude Haiku 4.5在SWE-bench Lite中的N=5个上下文密集型实例上评估了Focus。通过鼓励频繁压缩的积极提示，Focus实现了22.7%的令牌减少 (1490万 -> 1150万个令牌)，同时保持了相同的准确率 (3/5 = 60% 对两个智能体而言)。Focus平均每个任务执行6.0次自主压缩，在单个实例上最多节省了57%的令牌。我们证明了，如果给予适当的工具和提示，具有能力的模型可以自主地自我调节其上下文，从而为在不牺牲任务性能的情况下，实现成本敏感的智能系统开辟道路。

title: Enhancing Cloud Network Resilience via a Robust LLM-Empowered Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Framework
summary: While virtualization and resource pooling empower cloud networks with structural flexibility and elastic scalability, they inevitably expand the attack surface and challenge cyber resilience. Reinforcement Learning (RL)-based defense strategies have been developed to optimize resource deployment and isolation policies under adversarial conditions, aiming to enhance system resilience by maintaining and restoring network availability. However, existing approaches lack robustness as they require retraining to adapt to dynamic changes in network structure, node scale, attack strategies, and attack intensity. Furthermore, the lack of Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) support limits interpretability and flexibility. To address these limitations, we propose CyberOps-Bots, a hierarchical multi-agent reinforcement learning framework empowered by Large Language Models (LLMs). Inspired by MITRE ATT&CK's Tactics-Techniques model, CyberOps-Bots features a two-layer architecture: (1) An upper-level LLM agent with four modules--ReAct planning, IPDRR-based perception, long-short term memory, and action/tool integration--performs global awareness, human intent recognition, and tactical planning; (2) Lower-level RL agents, developed via heterogeneous separated pre-training, execute atomic defense actions within localized network regions. This synergy preserves LLM adaptability and interpretability while ensuring reliable RL execution. Experiments on real cloud datasets show that, compared to state-of-the-art algorithms, CyberOps-Bots maintains network availability 68.5% higher and achieves a 34.7% jumpstart performance gain when shifting the scenarios without retraining. To our knowledge, this is the first study to establish a robust LLM-RL framework with HITL support for cloud defense. We will release our framework to the community, facilitating the advancement of robust and autonomous defense in cloud networks.
tldr: The paper introduces CyberOps-Bots, a robust LLM-empowered multi-agent reinforcement learning framework for cloud defense, demonstrating significant improvements in network availability and jumpstart performance compared to existing methods without retraining.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种名为 CyberOps-Bots 的鲁棒 LLM 赋能多智能体强化学习框架用于云防御，与现有方法相比，该框架在网络可用性和启动性能方面均表现出显著提升，且无需重新训练。
summary_zh: 虚拟化和资源池化赋予云网络结构灵活性和弹性可扩展性，但同时也不可避免地扩大了攻击面并挑战了网络弹性。基于强化学习（RL）的防御策略已被开发出来，以优化对抗条件下的资源部署和隔离策略，旨在通过维持和恢复网络可用性来增强系统弹性。然而，现有的方法缺乏鲁棒性，因为它们需要重新训练才能适应网络结构、节点规模、攻击策略和攻击强度的动态变化。此外，缺乏人机回路（HITL）支持限制了解释性和灵活性。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了CyberOps-Bots，一个由大型语言模型（LLM）赋能的分层多智能体强化学习框架。受MITRE ATT&CK的战术-技术模型的启发，CyberOps-Bots具有两层架构：（1）一个上层LLM智能体，带有四个模块——ReAct规划、基于IPDRR的感知、长短期记忆和动作/工具集成——执行全局感知、人类意图识别和战术规划；（2）下层RL智能体，通过异构分离的预训练开发，在局部网络区域内执行原子防御动作。这种协同作用保留了LLM的适应性和可解释性，同时确保了可靠的RL执行。在真实云数据集上的实验表明，与最先进的算法相比，CyberOps-Bots在不重新训练的情况下面临场景切换时，维持网络可用性高出68.5%，并实现了34.7%的启动性能增益。据我们所知，这是第一个建立具有HITL支持的、用于云防御的鲁棒LLM-RL框架的研究。我们将向社区发布我们的框架，以促进云网络中鲁棒和自主防御的发展。

title: MEDVISTAGYM: A Scalable Training Environment for Thinking with Medical Images via Tool-Integrated Reinforcement Learning
summary: Vision language models (VLMs) achieve strong performance on general image understanding but struggle to think with medical images, especially when performing multi-step reasoning through iterative visual interaction. Medical VLMs often rely on static visual embeddings and single-pass inference, preventing models from re-examining, verifying, or refining visual evidence during reasoning. While tool-integrated reasoning offers a promising path forward, open-source VLMs lack the training infrastructure to learn effective tool selection, invocation, and coordination in multi-modal medical reasoning. We introduce MedVistaGym, a scalable and interactive training environment that incentivizes tool-integrated visual reasoning for medical image analysis. MedVistaGym equips VLMs to determine when and which tools to invoke, localize task-relevant image regions, and integrate single or multiple sub-image evidence into interleaved multimodal reasoning within a unified, executable interface for agentic training. Using MedVistaGym, we train MedVistaGym-R1 to interleave tool use with agentic reasoning through trajectory sampling and end-to-end reinforcement learning. Across six medical VQA benchmarks, MedVistaGym-R1-8B exceeds comparably sized tool-augmented baselines by 19.10% to 24.21%, demonstrating that structured agentic training--not tool access alone--unlocks effective tool-integrated reasoning for medical image analysis.
tldr: The paper introduces MedVistaGym, a reinforcement learning environment for training vision-language models to reason with medical images using external tools, achieving significant performance gains on medical VQA benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了MedVistaGym，一个强化学习环境，用于训练视觉-语言模型使用外部工具进行医学图像推理，并在医学VQA基准测试中实现了显著的性能提升。
summary_zh: 视觉语言模型（VLMs）在通用图像理解方面取得了不错的表现，但在处理医学图像时，特别是在通过迭代式视觉交互执行多步骤推理时，表现欠佳。医学视觉语言模型通常依赖于静态视觉嵌入和单次推理，这妨碍了模型在推理过程中重新检查、验证或提炼视觉证据。虽然工具集成推理提供了一个有前景的发展方向，但开源视觉语言模型缺乏训练基础设施，无法学习在多模态医学推理中进行有效的工具选择、调用和协调。我们引入了MedVistaGym，一个可扩展的交互式训练环境，旨在激励工具集成的视觉推理用于医学图像分析。MedVistaGym使视觉语言模型能够确定何时以及调用哪些工具，定位与任务相关的图像区域，并将单个或多个子图像证据整合到交错的多模态推理中，并在统一的可执行界面中进行具身智能体训练。 通过使用MedVistaGym，我们训练了MedVistaGym-R1，通过轨迹采样和端到端强化学习，将工具使用与具身智能体推理相结合。在六个医学视觉问答基准测试中，MedVistaGym-R1-8B超过了同等规模的工具增强基线19.10%到24.21%，表明结构化的具身智能体训练--而不是仅仅是工具访问--解锁了用于医学图像分析的有效工具集成推理。

title: Video Evidence to Reasoning Efficient Video Understanding via Explicit Evidence Grounding
summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) face a fundamental dilemma in video reasoning: they are caught between the prohibitive computational costs of verbose reasoning and the hallucination risks of efficient, ungrounded approaches. To resolve this, we introduce the Chain of Evidence (CoE), a novel framework that architecturally decouples and co-optimizes perceptual grounding and reasoning efficiency. CoE incorporates two core innovations: (1) A lightweight Evidence Grounding Module (EGM) that acts as a query-guided filter, dynamically identifying and extracting a compact set of high-fidelity visual evidence; and (2) An Evidence-Anchoring Protocol optimized via Reinforcement Learning. Crucially, we design a composite reward mechanism that enforces process alignment, compelling the model to strictly reference identified temporal anchors during deduction, thereby mitigating hallucinations. To enable this, we construct CoE-Instruct, a large-scale dataset (164k samples) featuring a novel dual-annotation schema for separate perception and reasoning supervision. Extensive experiments on five benchmarks, including Video-MME, MVBench, and VSI-Bench, demonstrate that CoE-enhanced models establish a new state-of-the-art. They significantly outperform existing methods in accuracy, proving CoE to be a powerful and practical paradigm for reliable video understanding.
tldr: The paper introduces Chain of Evidence (CoE), a new framework that decouples perceptual grounding and reasoning efficiency in video understanding by using an Evidence Grounding Module (EGM) and reinforcement learning with a composite reward mechanism to reduce hallucination. They achieve state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种名为证据链 (CoE) 的新框架，通过使用证据基础模块 (EGM) 和具有复合奖励机制的强化学习来解耦视频理解中的感知基础和推理效率，从而减少幻觉。他们在多个基准测试上实现了最先进的结果。
summary_zh: 大型视觉-语言模型(LVLMs)在视频推理中面临一个根本性的困境：它们一方面受限于详尽推理带来的高昂计算成本，另一方面又存在高效但缺乏依据的方法带来的幻觉风险。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了证据链(CoE)，一个新颖的框架，它在架构上解耦并共同优化了感知基础和推理效率。CoE包含两个核心创新：(1) 一个轻量级的证据基础模块(EGM)，它充当查询引导的过滤器，动态地识别并提取一组紧凑的高保真视觉证据；(2) 一个通过强化学习优化的证据锚定协议。至关重要的是，我们设计了一种综合奖励机制，该机制强制执行过程对齐，促使模型在演绎过程中严格参考已识别的时间锚点，从而减少幻觉。为了实现这一点，我们构建了CoE-Instruct，一个大规模数据集（包含164k个样本），其特点是采用了一种新颖的双重标注模式来分别进行感知和推理监督。在包括Video-MME、MVBench和VSI-Bench在内的五个基准测试上进行的大量实验表明，CoE增强的模型建立了新的最先进水平。它们在准确性方面显著优于现有方法，证明CoE是一种强大而实用的可靠视频理解范例。

title: Smooth Operator: Smooth Verifiable Reward Activates Spatial Reasoning Ability of Vision-Language Model
summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) face a critical bottleneck in achieving precise numerical prediction for 3D scene understanding. Traditional reinforcement learning (RL) approaches, primarily based on relative ranking, often suffer from severe reward sparsity and gradient instability, failing to effectively exploit the verifiable signals provided by 3D physical constraints. Notably, in standard GRPO frameworks, relative normalization causes "near-miss" samples (characterized by small but non-zero errors) to suffer from advantage collapse. This leads to a severe data utilization bottleneck where valuable boundary samples are discarded during optimization. To address this, we introduce the Smooth Numerical Reward Activation (SNRA) operator and the Absolute-Preserving GRPO (AP-GRPO) framework. SNRA employs a dynamically parameterized Sigmoid function to transform raw feedback into a dense, continuous reward continuum. Concurrently, AP-GRPO integrates absolute scalar gradients to mitigate the numerical information loss inherent in conventional relative-ranking mechanisms. By leveraging this approach, we constructed Numerical3D-50k, a dataset comprising 50,000 verifiable 3D subtasks. Empirical results indicate that AP-GRPO achieves performance parity with large-scale supervised methods while maintaining higher data efficiency, effectively activating latent 3D reasoning in VLMs without requiring architectural modifications.
tldr: This paper introduces a novel reinforcement learning approach, AP-GRPO, with a smooth reward activation (SNRA) operator to improve numerical prediction accuracy in Vision-Language Models for 3D scene understanding, demonstrated on a new dataset, Numerical3D-50k.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种新的强化学习方法 AP-GRPO，它带有平滑奖励激活 (SNRA) 算子，以提高视觉语言模型在 3D 场景理解中的数字预测准确性，并在一个新的数据集 Numerical3D-50k 上进行了演示。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言模型 (VLMs) 在实现三维场景理解的精确数值预测方面面临着一个关键瓶颈。传统的强化学习 (RL) 方法，主要基于相对排序，往往存在严重的奖励稀疏性和梯度不稳定问题，无法有效利用三维物理约束提供的可验证信号。值得注意的是，在标准 GRPO 框架中，相对归一化会导致“近失”样本（以小但非零的误差为特征）遭受优势崩溃。这导致了严重的数据利用瓶颈，即有价值的边界样本在优化过程中被丢弃。 为了解决这个问题，我们引入了平滑数值奖励激活 (SNRA) 算子和绝对值保持 GRPO (AP-GRPO) 框架。 SNRA 采用动态参数化的 Sigmoid 函数将原始反馈转换为密集的、连续的奖励连续体。 同时，AP-GRPO 集成了绝对标量梯度，以减轻传统相对排序机制中固有的数值信息损失。 通过利用这种方法，我们构建了 Numerical3D-50k，这是一个包含 50,000 个可验证 3D 子任务的数据集。 实验结果表明，AP-GRPO 在保持更高数据效率的同时，实现了与大规模监督方法相当的性能，有效地激活了 VLMs 中潜在的 3D 推理能力，而无需进行架构修改。

title: ViewMorpher3D: A 3D-aware Diffusion Framework for Multi-Camera Novel View Synthesis in Autonomous Driving
summary:
Autonomous driving systems rely heavily on multi-view images to ensure accurate perception and robust decision-making. To effectively develop and evaluate perception stacks and planning algorithms, realistic closed-loop simulators are indispensable. While 3D reconstruction techniques such as Gaussian Splatting offer promising avenues for simulator construction, the rendered novel views often exhibit artifacts, particularly in extrapolated perspectives or when available observations are sparse.
  We introduce ViewMorpher3D, a multi-view image enhancement framework based on image diffusion models, designed to elevate photorealism and multi-view coherence in driving scenes. Unlike single-view approaches, ViewMorpher3D jointly processes a set of rendered views conditioned on camera poses, 3D geometric priors, and temporally adjacent or spatially overlapping reference views. This enables the model to infer missing details, suppress rendering artifacts, and enforce cross-view consistency.
  Our framework accommodates variable numbers of cameras and flexible reference/target view configurations, making it adaptable to diverse sensor setups. Experiments on real-world driving datasets demonstrate substantial improvements in image quality metrics, effectively reducing artifacts while preserving geometric fidelity.
tldr: ViewMorpher3D is a multi-view image diffusion framework that enhances the photorealism and coherence of rendered driving scenes, particularly addressing artifacts in novel view synthesis for autonomous driving simulations.
tldr_zh: ViewMorpher3D是一个多视角图像扩散框架，旨在提升渲染驾驶场景的真实感和一致性，特别解决了自动驾驶模拟中新视角合成的伪影问题。
summary_zh:
自动驾驶系统高度依赖多视角图像，以确保精确的感知和稳健的决策。为了有效开发和评估感知堆栈和规划算法，逼真的闭环仿真器不可或缺。虽然高斯溅射等三维重建技术为仿真器构建提供了有前景的途径，但渲染的新视角通常会表现出伪影，尤其是在外推视角或可用观测稀疏时。

我们引入了 ViewMorpher3D，一个基于图像扩散模型的多视角图像增强框架，旨在提升驾驶场景中的照片真实感和多视角一致性。与单视角方法不同，ViewMorpher3D 联合处理一组渲染视图，并以相机姿态、三维几何先验以及时间上相邻或空间上重叠的参考视图为条件。这使得模型能够推断缺失的细节、抑制渲染伪影并强化跨视角一致性。

我们的框架可容纳可变数量的相机以及灵活的参考/目标视图配置，使其适应于各种传感器设置。在真实驾驶数据集上的实验表明，图像质量指标得到了显著提升，有效地减少了伪影，同时保留了几何保真度。

title: ShowUI-Aloha: Human-Taught GUI Agent
summary: Graphical User Interfaces (GUIs) are central to human-computer interaction, yet automating complex GUI tasks remains a major challenge for autonomous agents, largely due to a lack of scalable, high-quality training data. While recordings of human demonstrations offer a rich data source, they are typically long, unstructured, and lack annotations, making them difficult for agents to learn from.To address this, we introduce ShowUI-Aloha, a comprehensive pipeline that transforms unstructured, in-the-wild human screen recordings from desktop environments into structured, actionable tasks. Our framework includes four key components: A recorder that captures screen video along with precise user interactions like mouse clicks, keystrokes, and scrolls. A learner that semantically interprets these raw interactions and the surrounding visual context, translating them into descriptive natural language captions. A planner that reads the parsed demonstrations, maintains task states, and dynamically formulates the next high-level action plan based on contextual reasoning. An executor that faithfully carries out these action plans at the OS level, performing precise clicks, drags, text inputs, and window operations with safety checks and real-time feedback. Together, these components provide a scalable solution for collecting and parsing real-world human data, demonstrating a viable path toward building general-purpose GUI agents that can learn effectively from simply observing humans.
tldr: The paper introduces ShowUI-Aloha, a pipeline for building GUI agents by learning from human desktop screen recordings, using components for recording, learning, planning, and executing GUI actions.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了ShowUI-Aloha，一个通过学习人类桌面屏幕录像来构建GUI代理的流程，使用组件进行记录、学习、计划和执行GUI动作。
summary_zh: 图形用户界面（GUI）是人机交互的核心，然而，自动化复杂的GUI任务仍然是自主代理面临的主要挑战，这很大程度上是由于缺乏可扩展的高质量训练数据。人类演示录像提供了一种丰富的数据来源，但通常冗长、非结构化且缺乏标注，使得代理难以从中学习。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了ShowUI-Aloha，一个全面的流程，可以将桌面环境中来自真实场景的非结构化人类屏幕录像转化为结构化的、可执行的任务。我们的框架包括四个关键组件：一个记录器，用于捕获屏幕视频以及精确的用户交互，如鼠标点击、键盘敲击和滚动；一个学习器，用于语义化地解释这些原始交互和周围的视觉上下文，并将它们翻译成描述性的自然语言标题；一个规划器，用于读取解析后的演示，维护任务状态，并基于上下文推理动态地制定下一个高层次行动计划；一个执行器，用于在操作系统层面忠实地执行这些行动计划，通过安全检查和实时反馈执行精确的点击、拖动、文本输入和窗口操作。这些组件共同提供了一个可扩展的解决方案，用于收集和解析真实世界的人类数据，展示了一条构建通用GUI代理的可行路径，这些代理可以简单地通过观察人类来有效地学习。

title: Motion Focus Recognition in Fast-Moving Egocentric Video
summary: From Vision-Language-Action (VLA) systems to robotics, existing egocentric datasets primarily focus on action recognition tasks, while largely overlooking the inherent role of motion analysis in sports and other fast-movement scenarios. To bridge this gap, we propose a real-time motion focus recognition method that estimates the subject's locomotion intention from any egocentric video. Our approach leverages the foundation model for camera pose estimation and introduces system-level optimizations to enable efficient and scalable inference. Evaluated on a collected egocentric action dataset, our method achieves real-time performance with manageable memory consumption through a sliding batch inference strategy. This work makes motion-centric analysis practical for edge deployment and offers a complementary perspective to existing egocentric studies on sports and fast-movement activities.
tldr: This paper introduces a real-time motion focus recognition method for egocentric video, addressing the lack of motion analysis in existing egocentric datasets, particularly in sports and fast-movement activities by leveraging camera pose estimation and optimized inference for edge deployment.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种用于以自我为中心的视频的实时运动焦点识别方法，通过利用相机姿态估计和优化的推理技术，解决了现有以自我为中心的数据集中缺乏运动分析的问题，特别是在运动和快速运动活动中，并适用于边缘部署。
summary_zh: 从视觉-语言-动作（VLA）系统到机器人技术，现有的第一人称视角数据集主要侧重于动作识别任务，而很大程度上忽略了运动分析在体育运动和其他快速运动场景中的内在作用。为了弥补这一差距，我们提出了一种实时运动焦点识别方法，能够从任何第一人称视角视频中估计主体的运动意图。我们的方法利用基础模型进行相机位姿估计，并引入系统级优化，以实现高效且可扩展的推理。在一个收集的第一人称视角动作数据集上进行评估，我们的方法通过滑动批次推理策略实现了实时性能，并具有可控的内存消耗。这项工作使以运动为中心的分析能够实际应用于边缘部署，并为现有关于体育运动和快速运动活动的第一人称视角研究提供了一个互补的视角。

title: Efficient Visual Question Answering Pipeline for Autonomous Driving via Scene Region Compression
summary: Autonomous driving increasingly relies on Visual Question Answering (VQA) to enable vehicles to understand complex surroundings by analyzing visual inputs and textual queries. Currently, a paramount concern for VQA in this domain is the stringent requirement for fast latency and real-time processing, as delays directly impact real-world safety in this safety-critical application. However, current state-of-the-art VQA models, particularly large vision-language models (VLMs), often prioritize performance over computational efficiency. These models typically process dense patch tokens for every frame, leading to prohibitive computational costs (FLOPs) and significant inference latency, especially with long video sequences. This focus limits their practical deployment in real-time autonomous driving scenarios. To tackle this issue, we propose an efficient VLM framework for autonomous driving VQA tasks, SRC-Pipeline. It learns to compress early frame tokens into a small number of high-level tokens while retaining full patch tokens for recent frames. Experiments on autonomous driving video question answering tasks show that our approach achieves 66% FLOPs reduction while maintaining comparable performance, enabling VLMs to operate more effectively in real-time, safety-critical autonomous driving settings.
tldr: The paper introduces an efficient VLM framework (SRC-Pipeline) for autonomous driving VQA, which compresses early frame tokens to reduce FLOPs by 66% while maintaining comparable performance, enabling real-time operation.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种高效的VLM框架(SRC-Pipeline)，用于自动驾驶VQA。该框架通过压缩早期帧tokens，在保持可比性能的同时，将FLOPs 降低了 66%，从而实现实时运行。
summary_zh: 自动驾驶越来越依赖于视觉问答(VQA)技术，通过分析视觉输入和文本查询来使车辆理解复杂的周围环境。目前，该领域VQA的首要问题是对快速延迟和实时处理的严格要求，因为延迟直接影响这个安全关键应用中的实际安全。然而，当前最先进的VQA模型，特别是大型视觉-语言模型(VLMs)，通常优先考虑性能而非计算效率。这些模型通常处理每帧的密集patch tokens，导致巨大的计算成本(FLOPs)和显著的推理延迟，尤其是在处理长视频序列时。这种侧重限制了它们在实时自动驾驶场景中的实际部署。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种用于自动驾驶VQA任务的高效 VLM 框架，SRC-Pipeline。它学习将早期帧tokens压缩成少量高级tokens，同时保留最近帧的完整patch tokens。在自动驾驶视频问答任务上的实验表明，我们的方法在保持可比性能的同时，实现了66%的FLOPs 减少，使VLMs能够在实时、安全关键的自动驾驶环境中更有效地运行。

title: Offline Meta-Reinforcement Learning with Flow-Based Task Inference and Adaptive Correction of Feature Overgeneralization
summary: Offline meta-reinforcement learning (OMRL) combines the strengths of learning from diverse datasets in offline RL with the adaptability to new tasks of meta-RL, promising safe and efficient knowledge acquisition by RL agents. However, OMRL still suffers extrapolation errors due to out-of-distribution (OOD) actions, compromised by broad task distributions and Markov Decision Process (MDP) ambiguity in meta-RL setups. Existing research indicates that the generalization of the $Q$ network affects the extrapolation error in offline RL. This paper investigates this relationship by decomposing the $Q$ value into feature and weight components, observing that while decomposition enhances adaptability and convergence in the case of high-quality data, it often leads to policy degeneration or collapse in complex tasks. We observe that decomposed $Q$ values introduce a large estimation bias when the feature encounters OOD samples, a phenomenon we term ''feature overgeneralization''. To address this issue, we propose FLORA, which identifies OOD samples by modeling feature distributions and estimating their uncertainties. FLORA integrates a return feedback mechanism to adaptively adjust feature components. Furthermore, to learn precise task representations, FLORA explicitly models the complex task distribution using a chain of invertible transformations. We theoretically and empirically demonstrate that FLORA achieves rapid adaptation and meta-policy improvement compared to baselines across various environments.
tldr: The paper proposes FLORA, an offline meta-RL method that addresses feature overgeneralization in Q-value decomposition by modeling feature distributions and adaptively correcting feature components, achieving improved adaptation and meta-policy performance.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了FLORA，一种离线元强化学习方法，通过对特征分布进行建模并自适应地修正特征分量，解决了Q值分解中的特征过度泛化问题，从而提升了适应性和元策略性能。
summary_zh: 离线元强化学习（OMRL）结合了离线强化学习中从多样化数据集中学习的优势以及元强化学习对新任务的适应性，有望实现强化学习智能体的安全高效知识获取。然而，由于元强化学习设置中广泛的任务分布和马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）的歧义性，OMRL仍然因分布外（OOD）动作而遭受外推误差的影响。现有研究表明，$Q$网络的泛化性会影响离线强化学习中的外推误差。本文通过将$Q$值分解为特征和权重分量来研究这种关系，观察到虽然分解在高质量数据的情况下增强了适应性和收敛性，但在复杂任务中经常导致策略退化或崩溃。我们观察到，当特征遇到OOD样本时，分解后的$Q$值会引入较大的估计偏差，我们称之为“特征过度泛化”。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了FLORA，它通过对特征分布进行建模并估计其不确定性来识别OOD样本。FLORA集成了一种回报反馈机制来自适应地调整特征分量。此外，为了学习精确的任务表示，FLORA使用一系列可逆变换显式地对复杂的任务分布进行建模。我们在理论和实践中证明，与各种环境中的基线方法相比，FLORA实现了快速适应和元策略改进。

title: THETA: Triangulated Hand-State Estimation for Teleoperation and Automation in Robotic Hand Control
summary: The teleoperation of robotic hands is limited by the high costs of depth cameras and sensor gloves, commonly used to estimate hand relative joint positions (XYZ). We present a novel, cost-effective approach using three webcams for triangulation-based tracking to approximate relative joint angles (theta) of human fingers. We also introduce a modified DexHand, a low-cost robotic hand from TheRobotStudio, to demonstrate THETA's real-time application. Data collection involved 40 distinct hand gestures using three 640x480p webcams arranged at 120-degree intervals, generating over 48,000 RGB images. Joint angles were manually determined by measuring midpoints of the MCP, PIP, and DIP finger joints. Captured RGB frames were processed using a DeepLabV3 segmentation model with a ResNet-50 backbone for multi-scale hand segmentation. The segmented images were then HSV-filtered and fed into THETA's architecture, consisting of a MobileNetV2-based CNN classifier optimized for hierarchical spatial feature extraction and a 9-channel input tensor encoding multi-perspective hand representations. The classification model maps segmented hand views into discrete joint angles, achieving 97.18% accuracy, 98.72% recall, F1 Score of 0.9274, and a precision of 0.8906. In real-time inference, THETA captures simultaneous frames, segments hand regions, filters them, and compiles a 9-channel tensor for classification. Joint-angle predictions are relayed via serial to an Arduino, enabling the DexHand to replicate hand movements. Future research will increase dataset diversity, integrate wrist tracking, and apply computer vision techniques such as OpenAI-Vision. THETA potentially ensures cost-effective, user-friendly teleoperation for medical, linguistic, and manufacturing applications.
tldr: This paper introduces THETA, a cost-effective triangulation-based hand tracking system using three webcams and a CNN classifier to replicate human hand movements on a robotic hand, achieving high accuracy in joint angle prediction for teleoperation.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍THETA，一个经济高效的基于三角测量的手部跟踪系统，使用三个网络摄像头和一个CNN分类器来复制机器手上的人手运动，在关节角度预测方面实现了高精度，适用于远程操作。
